title: Request Construction, Method Semantics, Safety, and Idempotency · Go Client Systems · Program Defense
summary: A release board evaluating a changed infrastructure-provider contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for request construction, method semantics, safety, and idempotency, producing method-aware request builder. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Go 1.26 net/http method-aware request builder. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggi…
objectives: Construct a request with an intentional method, target, fields, body, context, and caller-owned policy. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Distinguish safe and idempotent method semantics from what one particular server happens to do. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Use GET and HEAD with conditional, range, cache, and body expectations that preserve their defined semantics. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Request Components · changed case · Quiz Request Components
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Go vendor-release candidate must repeat request G-2 after an uncertain timeout: GET status, POST job creation, PUT replacement, and PATCH transition are candidates. Classify safety, idempotency, body replay, and If-Match evidence before choosing. Competency probe: Construct a request with an intentional method, target, fields, body, context, and caller-owned policy. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Which action demonstrates Request Components without dropping earlier contracts?
